select_when: Trust or relationship depth must be built before a difficult ask, negotiation, or sale can be evaluated seriously.
The value offered first is real, relevant, and costly enough to signal sincere investment rather than manipulation theater.
The goal is durable goodwill and mutual commitment instead of a one-off extraction of compliance.
A recommendation needs to separate relationship-building moves from psychological pressure that could bypass informed evaluation.
avoid_when: Gifts, favors, or access are becoming a distraction from the substance, economics, or ethics of the underlying decision.
The model is being used to chase assent without ensuring the other side has actually judged the merits with deliberative thought.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on value-first trust building and the line between sincere reciprocity and manipulation-driven compliance.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor persuasion sprawl beyond the raw source's stronger relationship, commitment, and anti-manipulation material.
Did not add higher-order composition fields because this batch remains inside the frozen Wave 1 to Wave 3 contracts.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans consulting, ownership design, and influence ethics.

select_when.note: The source is explicit that reciprocity is strongest when real value builds trust and mutual commitment before a difficult ask.

avoid_when.note: The danger blocks are explicit that reciprocity can become manipulation or obligation without informed evaluation.

input_type.note: The source clearly activates on trust-building influence problems, while the compact type label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw source yields a value-first relationship approach, but the typed output label is a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: The model is naturally diagnostic of relationship dynamics and causal in how value-first exchange changes later assent, while the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
